extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ Primož won stage 1 after late regrouping, outsprinting Thomas and Betancur despite struggling on climbs throughout the day
tactical_patterns: Thomas attacked 900m out but commitment too early; Betancur wheel-sucking until final 300m neutralized his advantage completely.
Buchmann hunted intermediate sprint points despite starting 15 seconds down, signaling GC ambition through bonus seconds strategy early.
Select group formation on lumpy terrain allowed GC riders to contest stage win rather than specialists; climb difficulty overestimated.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: attacked 900m out but couldn't win first race since Tour de France, suggesting post-Grand Tour sprint sharpness issue
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: admitted struggling on climbs but won sprint, indicating recovery form not full strength after Tirreno break
rider_signals.BETANCUR Carlos: positive: marked Thomas perfectly in final km despite being only 2 seconds ahead on GC, aggressive defense tactics
rider_signals.BUCHMANN Emanuel: positive: chasing intermediate sprint points from 15 seconds down shows confidence in limiting losses on later MTFs
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: neutral: mentioned as strong for team but absent from finale despite TT prowess suggesting stage profile unsuitability
